prompt: Explain how Vite creates a development server, resolves configuration, wires the plugin container, tracks modules, and transforms source during requests.

expected_symbols.name: createServer
expected_symbols.why: Main dev server construction entry point.

expected_symbols.name: resolveConfig
expected_symbols.why: Resolves user and default configuration before server wiring.

expected_symbols.name: createPluginContainer
expected_symbols.why: Builds the plugin execution surface used by the server.

expected_symbols.name: ModuleGraph
expected_symbols.why: Tracks module relationships and transform state.

expected_symbols.name: transformRequest
expected_symbols.why: Transforms requested modules through the server pipeline.

expected_claims.text: createServer is the development server construction entry point.
Configuration is resolved before the server finishes wiring plugins and middleware.
The plugin container is the server-facing mechanism for running plugin hooks.
The module graph tracks module relationships and transform state for dev-server requests.
Source transformation for module requests is handled by the transform request pipeline.

forbidden_claims.text: The dev server transforms source without using resolved configuration or plugins.
The module graph is only a production build artifact.
